g.data-narration: Welcome to System V IPC - the fundamental inter-process communication framework in Unix systems.
IPC allows processes to communicate, share data, and synchronize their activities safely and efficiently.
System V provides three primary IPC mechanisms: Message Queues, Shared Memory, and Semaphores.
The ftok() function generates unique IPC keys from pathname and project ID for identifying IPC objects.
msgget() creates or accesses a message queue, returning a unique msgqid identifier.
msgsnd sends messages to the queue; msgrcv receives them based on message type filtering.

g.text: size_t msgsz, long msgtyp, int msgflg);
msgtyp: 0 = first any, >0 = first of type, <0 = type ≤ |msgtyp|

g.data-narration: Message structures must have long mtype as the first field, followed by the message data.
shmget() creates a shared memory segment that multiple processes can access for fast data exchange.

g.text: Process A <--- Shared Memory ---> Process B
Direct memory access - No data copying!

g.data-narration: shmat() attaches shared memory to process address space; shmdt() detaches it when done.

g.text: ⚠ Returns (void*)-1 on failure, NOT NULL! Always check for -1.

g.data-narration: shmctl() performs control operations on shared memory including IPC_STAT, IPC_SET, and IPC_RMID.
g.text: int shmctl(int shmid, int cmd, struct shmid_ds *buf);

g.data-narration: Semaphores are synchronization primitives. semget() creates a semaphore array with multiple semaphores.
semop() performs atomic semaphore operations: negative sem_op for wait(P), positive for signal(V).
g.text: int semop(int semid, struct sembuf *sops, size_t nsops);

g.data-narration: The sembuf structure defines semaphore operations with sem_num, sem_op, and sem_flg fields.

g.text: int semctl(int semid, int semnum, int cmd, ...);

g.data-narration: All IPC mechanisms share common control operations: IPC_STAT, IPC_SET, and IPC_RMID.
Use ipcs to view IPC objects and ipcrm to remove them from the system.
Remember these 10 essential functions for the university examination: ftok, msgget, msgsnd, msgrcv, msgctl, shmget, shmat, shmdt, shmctl, semget, semop, semctl.
Key exam tips: ftok generates keys, msgtype filtering in msgrcv, shmat returns -1 not NULL, negative sem_op is wait operation.

g.text: msg structure: long mtype MUST be first

g.data-narration: Compare IPC mechanisms: Message queues are buffered, shared memory is fastest, semaphores provide synchronization.
